name: Early Intervention for ME (Birth to Three)
description: Maine's early intervention program helping children from birth to three achieve developmental milestones. Family-focused specialists show caregivers how to incorporate learning opportunities into everyday activities. Free assessments and evaluations through 9 regional sites statewide.

name: The Arc Maine
description: Affiliated chapter of The Arc promoting and protecting the rights of people with intellectual and developmental disabilities in Maine, supporting full inclusion and participation in the community throughout their lifetimes.

name: Special Olympics Maine
description: Year-round sports training and competition program serving over 5,000 athletes with intellectual disabilities. Offers 90+ events each year at local, state, national, and international levels across multiple sports including skiing, track and field, and bowling.
address: 525 Main Street, Unit D, South Portland, ME 04106

name: Disability Rights Maine
description: Maine's designated Protection and Advocacy agency, providing no-cost advocacy and legal services to people with disabilities who have experienced a violation of their legal or civil rights. Employs 36 staff including 13 attorneys.

name: Maine Bureau of Rehabilitation Services - Division of Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Department of Labor program helping people with physical, mental, or emotional disabilities find and keep a job. Services include career exploration, training, job placement, and tools/equipment needed for employment, all provided at no cost.
ageRange: Working age adults and transition-age youth

name: NAMI Maine
description: Dedicated to building better lives for 1 in 4 Mainers affected by mental illness through support, education, and advocacy. Offers the NAMI Helpline, educational programming, workshops, conferences, support groups, and a teen text line.

name: Maine CITE (Community Inclusion through Technology)
description: Statewide program helping people with disabilities increase independence through assistive technology. Provides free device demonstrations, short-term loans, equipment reuse, information, and training. The mPower Loan Program offers affordable loans for AT purchases and home modifications.

name: ABLE ME
description: Maine's ABLE savings program administered by Bangor Savings Bank, helping eligible individuals with disabilities save for qualified disability expenses without jeopardizing SSI, Medicaid, or other benefits. FDIC-insured checking account with no annual set fee and no restrictions on disbursements.

name: Barbara Bush Children's Hospital - Developmental-Behavioral Pediatrics
description: Part of MaineHealth Maine Medical Center, providing comprehensive behavioral and developmental services for infants, children, teens, and young adults aged 0-21 throughout Maine. Team includes board-certified developmental and behavioral specialists and neuropsychologists.

name: Maine Adaptive Sports & Recreation
description: The largest year-round adaptive recreation program in Maine since 1982, offering alpine skiing, snowboarding, Nordic skiing, cycling, golf, paddling, hiking, and more. All lessons are 100% free including lift tickets, equipment, and instruction. Supported by over 400 trained volunteers.
